topic: llama-stack vs Ollama vs vLLM: Which Local LLM Stack Should You Use in 2026

key_points: Detailed benchmark comparison of llama.cpp, Ollama, and vLLM with hardware-specific results
Focuses on throughput, latency, and use-case breakdowns for 2026
Recommends Ollama for dev, vLLM for production
Performance benchmarks: vLLM 793 tok/s vs Ollama 41 tok/s at peak
p99 latency comparison: vLLM 80ms vs Ollama 673ms
50 concurrent users: vLLM 6x throughput advantage over Ollama
Comparison of four LLM inference engines including TGI
Architecture deep-dive on PagedAttention vs sequential processing
Use case matrix for different deployment scenarios
Meta's llama-stack as orchestration/API layer over inference providers
Standardized APIs for inference, agents, safety, vector storage, evaluation
Enterprise use cases and provider-agnostic design philosophy
vLLM V1 delivers 1.7x throughput over V0 with FlashAttention 3
920 tok/s under 50 concurrent users on standard GPU
4,741 tok/s at 100 concurrent requests on 2x H100 GPUs

stats.fact: vLLM achieves ~793 tokens/s vs Ollama's ~41 tokens/s at peak throughput (19x difference)
stats.source: Red Hat benchmark, August 2025 (referenced in SitePoint 2026)

stats.fact: vLLM p99 latency at peak: 80ms vs Ollama 673ms (8x lower latency)
stats.source: SitePoint Ollama vs vLLM Performance Benchmark 2026

stats.fact: At 50 concurrent users, vLLM delivers 6x total throughput with p99 latency under 3 seconds vs Ollama's 24.7-second p99
vLLM on 2x H100 GPUs: 4,741 tok/s at 100 concurrent requests
vLLM V1 delivers 1.7x throughput over V0, with FlashAttention 3 and prefix caching at <1% overhead
Ollama Cloud launched in public beta January 15, 2026 enabling cloud inference via 'ollama serve --cloud'
llama-stack released April 8, 2026 as OpenAI-compatible API server with pluggable providers (Ollama, vLLM, OpenAI, Fireworks)
vLLM latest release: v0.19.0 (April 2026), expanding Blackwell GPU support
vLLM+Ollama integration achieves 1,200 concurrent inference requests/hour vs Ollama native 280/hour (4.3x improvement)
stats.source: markaicode.com vLLM with Ollama Integration Guide 2026

angles: These three tools operate at different layers of the stack — llama-stack is an orchestration API (not an inference engine), while Ollama and vLLM are inference engines. Most comparisons miss this distinction.
The '2026 answer' is actually a combination: use llama-stack as the API layer, Ollama for dev/local work, and vLLM for production — they're complements, not competitors.
llama-stack's provider-agnostic design means your app code doesn't change when you switch from Ollama (dev) to vLLM (prod), solving a major pain point for AI teams.
vLLM's PagedAttention is the key technical differentiator: OS-style virtual memory for KV cache enables 2-4x more concurrent capacity than static allocation used by Ollama.
Ollama's new 'Ollama Cloud' (Jan 2026) blurs the local vs. cloud boundary — developers can push models to globally distributed endpoints while keeping the same CLI workflow.

llama-stack's Responses API supports server-side agentic orchestration with tool calling, MCP server integration, and built-in RAG in a single API call — functionality Ollama/vLLM don't natively provide.
For Apple Silicon users in 2026, neither Ollama nor vLLM is clearly best — MLX-backed Ollama and vllm-mlx compete, with tradeoffs depending on model size and Metal GPU utilization.
